themes: MCPS school rating system mechanics | Listener wants to know how the new score is calculated and what scores Rockville schools received
Friedson's line-item fiscal record | Final budget yes/no is not enough; listener wants vote-by-vote on individual budget items
Wootton High School closure timeline | Listener wants to know who decides, when, and how Board of Education members got their seats
Career-long voting records, not just recent sessions | Listener has flagged that recent roll-calls miss older positions, especially for Kramer 2007-present
Sanctuary policy opposition within Democratic primary | Listener wants to know which D primary candidates would relax sanctuary, not just oppose Trump
open_questions: What is the new MCPS rating methodology and how is each Rockville school graded under it?
Is Andrew Friedson actually the fiscal-restraint candidate when measured by individual line-item votes?
Why is MCPS proposing to close/relocate Wootton High School and what happens next on the timeline?
What is Ben Kramer's full historical voting record back to 2007 on consumer protection, judiciary, immigration, and education funding?
Among the five+ Democratic primary candidates on the ballot, which one (if any) actually opposes the existing sanctuary framework?
underserved_topics: Side-by-side ballot matrix across every Democratic primary candidate on the listener's actual ballot (Exec, At-Large, D7, MD-8, House D19, Senate D19)
Pre-political biographies and old press of candidates whose recent record is well-covered but whose deeper career arc is not
Specific votes on individual MCPS budget items by line, not just total budget yes/no
The Wootton timeline itself — when does the Board actually vote, what is the procedural path
summary: The listener is a first-time Democratic primary voter in Rockville. They want career-long records, line-item votes, and named answers on sanctuary opposition, fiscal restraint, and the Wootton question. Recurring frustration: deep-dive promises that deliver shallow recent-roll-call reads. They want old news and full history, not just the last session.
avoid_list: 'Thirty-X days out from the Maryland Democratic primary' as the cold-open device — used in podcast_2026-05-16 ep1, 2026-05-17 ep1, 2026-05-18 ep1, 2026-05-19 ep1
'Montgomery County native' as the framing hook — used heavily in 2026-05-16 Friedson ep1 and revisited 2026-05-18 Hidayat ep1
'My Seven Black Fathers' as the framing device for any candidate other than Will Jawando — used in 2026-05-19 ep1
'Anniston, Alabama / Donoho School / Vaughn Stewart Junior as mayor' as the formative-experience device for any candidate other than Vaughn Stewart — used in 2026-05-20 ep1
'Born in Trenton, between Atlantic City and Philadelphia / adopted as an infant from a teenage mom in a convent' as origin device for any candidate other than Dawn Luedtke — used in 2026-05-17 ep1
'First Muslim officer of the Montgomery County Police Department' as identity-formation device for any candidate other than Sharif Hidayat — used in 2026-05-18 ep1

'Pre-political biography is thinner in the public record / no Wikipedia entry adds independent verification' as a candidate-specific qualifier when reusable on other candidates — used in 2026-05-16 ep1
Reference to 'My Seven Black Fathers' memoir or Reid Temple A.M.E. Church for any candidate other than Jawando — used in 2026-05-19 ep1
ICE Out Act / Trust Act / DHS officer screening bill as the immigration-record framing hook — used in 2026-05-21 Glass ep3 and ep4; can be referenced but not lead-framed in next-week candidates
'Run the buses, teach the kids, pick up the trash' tagline as a candidate-pitch device — used in 2026-05-21 Glass ep1, ep2, and ep4
